title: Test custom prefix
close_reason: test complete

title: Event visualizations: CLI and TUI tracing
description: Add CLI and TUI visualizations on top of .trx/events.jsonl so users can trace what has been done. Surface AGENT_CTX (user, harness, session, model, etc.) where available. Children cover individual CLI commands and TUI views.
close_reason: Event visualizations shipped: trx log/sessions/stats CLI + TUI activity/sessions/follow

title: trx log: chronological activity feed (CLI)
description: git-log-style chronological feed across issues. Group by session (platform_session_id or harness_session_id), show timestamp, action (color-coded), issue id, note, indented field diffs. Render a dimmed AGENT_CTX line ([harness · session_name · model]) when fields are present. Flags: --since, --until, --user, --session, --action, --issue, --limit, --no-group (flat mode).

title: trx sessions: list + drilldown (CLI)
description: trx sessions: one row per distinct session_id with time range, user, harness, model, event count, top issues touched. trx sessions <id>: full trace for that session (all events). Flags: --since, --until, --user, --limit.
close_reason: trx sessions: list view + per-session drilldown with AGENT_CTX, JSON output

title: trx stats: counts + sparkline/heatmap (CLI)
description: trx stats: aggregate counts of events with ASCII sparkline by day, plus breakdowns by user/harness/action. Flags: --since, --until, --by day|hour|user|harness|action, --weeks N. Reuse filter_events helper.

description: Add an Activity pane (or toggle in details pane) showing the selected issue's event timeline: timestamp, action (colored), note, field diffs. Press 'v' to toggle a foldable AGENT_CTX block (user/harness/session/model). Scrollable with j/k.
close_reason: TUI Activity pane: 'T' toggles right pane to event timeline; 'v' expands AGENT_CTX

close_reason: TUI Sessions view: 'S' swaps middle pane to sessions list; right pane shows session trace

title: TUI: follow/live-tail mode for events
description: Live-tail mode: a 'follow' toggle that re-reads .trx/events.jsonl on tick (1-2s) and shows newest events in an activity feed. Useful when an agent runs in another pane. Could be invoked as trx-tui follow or as a key toggle inside the TUI.
close_reason: TUI follow mode: 'F' enables ~2s tail of events.jsonl with ● follow indicator

title: AGENT_CTX support + drop CRDT, add event log
description: Implement AGENT_CTX env contract (../schemas/agent-context-env). Drop v1/v2 split — single JSONL store, transparent on-read migration from Automerge. Add append-only events.jsonl to track which sessions touched which issues, when, and what they did. New commands: trx info (effective context), trx history <id>, trx events. Versionless storage with auto-detection.

title: Rip out v1/v2 storage split, single JSONL store
description: Remove StorageVersion enum, version field in Config, UnifiedStore v1/v2 dispatch, CrdtStore code path. Store becomes the single canonical implementation (JSONL). The 'trx migrate' command becomes obsolete — either remove or repurpose. Delete crdt_store.rs and unified_store.rs once callers are updated.
close_reason: Already implemented and shipped in 0.5.0/0.6.0; erroneously reopened 2026-05-30 by 'Prevent Concurrent Trx Writes' mass-reopen. Verified against code.

title: Transparent Automerge -> JSONL auto-migration on read
description: Detect Automerge bytes in .trx/ on store open. Read into in-memory Issue list, write JSONL on next mutation (atomic temp+rename). Never mutate disk on read. Hard error with clear message on unrecognized format. No version flag, no migrate command — pure structural detection.
close_reason: Already implemented and shipped in 0.5.0/0.6.0; erroneously reopened 2026-05-30 by 'Prevent Concurrent Trx Writes' mass-reopen. Verified against code.

title: agent_ctx module + trx info command
description: New trx-core::agent_ctx module: read AGENT_CTX_* env vars defensively, return AgentCtx struct of Option<String> fields per the schema (../schemas/agent-context-env). Never panic on missing/malformed. Add 'trx info' command that prints effective context (and basic store info: path, issue count, event count) — debug-friendly output.
close_reason: agent_ctx module + trx info command done

title: Event log: .trx/events.jsonl + emission on mutations
description: Append-only events.jsonl. Event { id, issue_id, action, timestamp, user_id, platform, harness, platform_session_id, harness_session_id, request_id, correlation_id, workspace_id, model, changes[], note }. Emit on create/update/close/reopen/delete/dep_added/dep_removed. Auto-fill created_by from AGENT_CTX_USER_ID and append session ids to issue.sessions (deduped) when AGENT_CTX present.
close_reason: Already implemented and shipped in 0.5.0/0.6.0; erroneously reopened 2026-05-30 by 'Prevent Concurrent Trx Writes' mass-reopen. Verified against code.

title: trx history <id> and trx events query commands
description: trx history <id>: timeline of events for one issue. trx events with --session <id>, --user <id>, --since <date>, --action <name>, --issue <id> filters for cross-issue audit. Reuse the existing --json flag.
close_reason: Already implemented and shipped in 0.5.0/0.6.0; erroneously reopened 2026-05-30 by 'Prevent Concurrent Trx Writes' mass-reopen. Verified against code.

title: Agent CLI ergonomics pass
description: Make trx more ergonomic for agent use. Concrete items: (1) Retire/fix 'trx dep add --blocks' which is lying — its message says 'id now blocks X' but the data records 'id depends on X'. Either remove dep add/rm entirely (block/unblock are correct) or invert dep_add to match its name. (2) Bulk operations: trx update accepting multiple IDs; trx close accepting multiple IDs. (3) trx ready accepts filters (--type, --priority, --label, --assignee). (4) trx show should display recent events for the issue (post trx-40mg.5). (5) Consistent flag short forms (currently mixes -p create-priority and -P list-priority). (6) trx schema includes the Event schema.

title: Test issue for TUI sync verification
close_reason: Disposable test scaffold for TUI sync verification; no longer needed.

title: Drop V1 (issues.jsonl) storage backend; CRDT-only
description:
## Why

V2 (CRDT, automerge files under .trx/crdt/) has been the default for a while and is the only format new stores create (StorageVersion::default() == V2). Keeping V1 alive forces every method on trx-core to go through the UnifiedStore enum split (V1(Store) | V2(CrdtStore)), adding boilerplate without value:

- crates/trx-core/src/unified_store.rs: every method matches on the variant
- crates/trx-core/src/store.rs: full V1 implementation kept in lockstep
- migration code (migrate_v1_to_v2, rollback_v2_to_v1) carries dead weight once V1 is gone

CRDT is also the right call architecturally: concurrent writers across CLI / TUI / MCP / external embedders (oqto sidebar, future agents) need it, and the merge_conflict / resolve_conflicts paths in CrdtStore already exist for this.

## Plan

Two-release deprecation:

1. **This release (deprecation)**: keep V1 readable, but auto-migrate any v1 store to v2 on first open. Print a one-time warning. No behavior change for v2 users.
2. **Next release (removal)**: delete crates/trx-core/src/store.rs (V1), collapse UnifiedStore down to a single struct (or alias to CrdtStore), remove migrate_v1_to_v2 / rollback_v2_to_v1 / StorageVersion enum, simplify config (drop storage_version field, defaulting reads to v2 if missing).

After removal:
- UnifiedStore becomes either a thin re-export of CrdtStore or is removed in favor of using CrdtStore directly
- open_at(root) (see trx-bwt9) becomes a single impl
- ~ a few hundred lines removed from trx-core
- CLI flags like 'init --v1' (if any) go away

## Open questions

- Are there known v1 stores in the wild we should migrate before cutting? (CI / dogfood repos)
- Should the deprecation release ship migration as opt-out (auto on open) or opt-in (explicit 'trx migrate')?

## Depends on

trx-bwt9 (open_at) lands first so embedders aren't disrupted by the V1 removal.
close_reason: Superseded by trx-40mg: decision reversed — JSONL is canonical, CRDT (v2) is being dropped instead. CRDT machinery doesn't earn its keep for an issue tracker; rare concurrent same-issue edits don't justify Automerge's opacity, complexity, or maintenance cost.

title: Beads migration: Import and purge
description: Commands to import existing beads issues and purge beads from repos. Critical for adoption - must be seamless migration path.

title: Import: trx import command for beads JSONL
description: trx import .beads/issues.jsonl [--prefix PREFIX]. Read beads JSONL, convert to trx format (mostly compatible), write to .trx/issues.jsonl. Handle ID prefix conversion (bd-xxx -> trx-xxx or keep original). Report stats: imported, skipped, errors.

title: Purge: trx purge-beads command
description: trx purge-beads [--force]. Remove .beads/ directory, daemon socket, registry entries. Warn if not imported first. --force skips confirmation.

title: Migration docs and byt integration
description: Document migration path in README. Update byt to support trx alongside beads. Add byt tools install trx.

title: Feature: ergonomic dependency commands with explicit direction (block/by)
description:
Current 'trx dep add <id> --blocks <other>' is correct but cognitively awkward during fast planning.

Request:
- Add directional aliases with explicit wording:
  - trx dep block <blocked-id> --by <blocker-id>
  - trx dep unblock <blocked-id> --by <blocker-id>
- Keep existing dep add/rm for backwards compatibility (or migrate with clear deprecation).

Also requested:
- Allow multiple blockers in one call: --by a,b,c
- Show concise confirmation: '<blocked> now blocked by <a,b,c>'

Acceptance criteria:
- New command aliases implemented and documented.
- Works with --json output and scripting.
- Existing dep commands remain functional.
close_reason: Implemented: trx dep block/unblock --by with comma-separated multiple blockers

title: Add first-class readiness lanes for agent/human issue workflow
description:
Agent workflows need a repo-independent way to distinguish "ready for an AFK agent" from "needs shaping/context/human review". Today this is usually encoded as ad-hoc labels or prose, while trx already has real statuses and dependency blocking.

- `status`: open, in_progress, blocked, closed, tombstone
- dependencies: `trx dep block/unblock/tree`; `trx ready` lists open unblocked work
- labels exist as generic strings but are not a stable workflow vocabulary

Add a first-class optional `readiness` (or `lane`) field separate from status and dependencies.

Suggested values:
- `needs_shaping` — issue exists but problem/approach/acceptance criteria are not ready
- `needs_context` — missing logs/repro/decision/environment info; not necessarily dependency-blocked
- `agent_ready` — fully specified and suitable for AFK agent pickup
- `human_review` — needs human/product/architecture judgment or manual action

Do **not** model blocking as a lane; keep structural blocking in `trx dep block/unblock` and status `blocked` if that remains useful.

- `trx update <id> --readiness agent_ready`
- `trx list --readiness agent_ready`
- `trx ready --readiness agent_ready`
- `trx next --for agent` => open + unblocked + readiness=agent_ready + priority order
- `trx next --for human` => open + unblocked + readiness=human_review (or needs_shaping) + priority order
- JSON/API/TUI expose and filter readiness.

This should be easy to add without breaking existing repos if implemented as an optional/defaulted field:
- Add `readiness: Option<Readiness>` to `Issue` with `#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`.
- Old `.trx/issues.jsonl` lines deserialize with `readiness = None`.
- Existing statuses, labels, dependencies, and `trx ready` behavior remain unchanged unless users opt into readiness filters.
- Existing labels stay generic; no migration required.
- Event log can record field changes as normal update events.
- API request structs can add optional readiness fields with serde default.

Potential compatibility gotchas:
- TUI filters currently include status/labels/ready_only; add readiness without changing defaults.
- Export/rendering should omit absent readiness to keep old output compact.
- If `Status::Blocked` is retained, document how it differs from dependency-blocked and readiness values.

- Existing repos without readiness continue to list/show/update/ready with identical behavior.
- New issues default to no readiness unless config chooses a default.
- CLI can set/clear readiness.
- list/ready/API/TUI can filter by readiness.
- Documentation explains status vs dependency blocking vs readiness lanes.

title: trx-core: Core library implementation
description: Implement the core trx library with minimal issue model, JSONL storage, and graph operations. Must be beads-viewer compatible for dependency visualization.

title: Issue data model with beads-compatible JSONL format
description: Implement Issue struct with ~20 fields (vs beads' ~117). Must serialize to beads-compatible JSONL for beads-viewer compatibility. Include: id, title, description, status, priority, issue_type, labels, timestamps, dependencies array.

title: JSONL store - read/write issues without SQLite
description: Implement Store struct that reads/writes .trx/issues.jsonl directly. No SQLite, no daemon. Operations: load_all, save_all, get, create, update, delete (tombstone). Must handle concurrent access gracefully.

title: Dependency graph with cycle detection and ready-work analysis
description: Implement IssueGraph using petgraph for: cycle detection, topological sort, ready work (issues with no open blockers), critical path analysis. Should support beads-viewer's PageRank-style prioritization.

title: Hash-based ID generation for conflict-free merges
description: Implement ID generation using UUID+timestamp hash. Format: trx-xxxx (4 alphanumeric). Support hierarchical IDs for children: trx-xxxx.1.2. Include parse/validate utilities.

title: TUI uses Store instead of UnifiedStore, causing data inconsistency with V2 storage
close_reason: Fixed by updating TUI to use UnifiedStore which auto-detects V1/V2 storage

title: Visual event views: heatmap, swimlane, TUI dashboard
description: Tabular sparklines weren't cool enough. Add: (1) trx heatmap — GitHub-style calendar grid (weeks × weekdays) with colored intensity cells. (2) trx swimlane — issue × time grid where each cell shows the most-impactful action color at that bucket, with a legend. (3) TUI Dashboard mode (key D) — full-screen view combining calendar heatmap, sparkline, ranked sessions/issues, and a live event tail.
close_reason: Shipped trx heatmap, trx swimlane, and TUI Dashboard mode (D key). Heatmap is a GitHub-style 13-week calendar grid with Unicode intensity blocks; swimlane is an issue × time matrix with action-priority resolution per bucket and a legend; TUI Dashboard combines a calendar heatmap, ranked by-action / by-user bars, a top-sessions panel, and a live event tail with follow support.

title: Add trx sync options for message and dry-run
description: Support --message to set commit message and --dry-run or --no-commit to inspect staged changes without committing.
close_reason: Added --dry-run and --no-commit to sync

title: Feature: add trx plan import for epic+children scaffolding from Markdown/YAML
description:
Add a high-level command to create an epic and children from a structured plan file in one shot.

Example:
- trx plan import docs/architecture/backend-target-tree.md --epic "..."
- or trx plan import plan.yaml

Benefits:
- Removes repetitive manual issue creation for large refactors.
- Keeps issue descriptions/acceptance criteria consistent with source docs.

Suggested behavior:
- Parse sections into child issue title/type/priority/description.
- Optional --dry-run to preview generated issues and dependencies.
- Optional --link-doc path to append canonical reference automatically.

Acceptance criteria:
- Can create 1 epic + N children in one command.
- Supports dependency declarations in source spec.
- Provides idempotency mode (skip/merge existing by title).
close_reason: Implemented: trx plan import for Markdown and JSON with --dry-run, auto-parenting

title: Add open_at(root) constructors to embed trx-core in process
close_reason: Added open_at(root) constructors to Store, CrdtStore, and UnifiedStore. open() now delegates to open_at after find_root.

close_reason: Description accepts '-' for stdin in create and update

title: TUI: add adaptive layout modes for wide, medium, and narrow terminals

title: TUI: make sidebar optional/collapsible for tree and saved-view contexts

title: TUI: add issue editing via VISUAL/EDITOR with safe terminal restore

title: TUI: add quick inline editors for status, priority, type, labels, and assignee

title: trx list --epic <id> to show all children/descendants of an epic

title: print status (open/closed/etc) when running trx list
Add --clear for update fields

title: Implement dependency tree rendering
description: trx dep tree currently prints '(not yet implemented)'. Add traversal to display blockers/children with type labels and detect cycles.
close_reason: Implemented in v0.3.0: trx dep tree with recursive rendering, cycle detection, and --json output

title: Add date range filters (--created-after/--created-before) to list
description: Issues have created_at/updated_at timestamps but list has no date range filter. Cannot say 'issues created this week'. Add --created-after and --created-before flags that filter on created_at. Accept relative formats ('1 week', '2 days') and ISO dates.
close_reason: Added --created-after/--created-before with ISO and relative date parsing

title: Show reverse dependencies in trx show
description: Add optional output (e.g., --reverse or default section) that lists issues blocked by this one and parent/child relationships, so epics can show their children.
close_reason: Implemented: trx show displays Children and Blocks sections; --json includes parent, blocked_by, blocks, children fields

title: Add --label filter to list
description: Issues have labels but list cannot filter by them. Add --label flag that filters issues containing the specified label. Support multiple --label flags for AND filtering. Labels are the cross-tool connector for unified search across hstry/mmry/trx.
close_reason: Added --label filter with AND semantics

title: Expose blockers in trx ready output
description: When an issue is blocked, show which dependencies are open to explain why it isn't ready.
close_reason: Implemented: trx ready now shows blocked issues section with blocker IDs

title: Add cross-repo issue search
description: Each .trx/ store is isolated per repo. Cannot search issues across all repos. Add a --all-repos flag (or trx search --global) that discovers .trx/ directories in sibling repos and merges results. Needed for cross-project queries like 'all open authentication bugs across all repos'.
close_reason: Added trx search --all-repos for cross-repo discovery

title: Feature: trx create/update support external editor workflow for long descriptions
description:
For long issue authoring, add editor-based workflow similar to git commit messages.

Request:
- trx create "Title" --edit
- trx update <id> --edit
- Open nvim with prefilled template fields (description, scope, acceptance criteria).

Why:
- Reduces shell escaping friction and improves quality of issue descriptions.
- Faster than inline -d for multi-paragraph content.

Acceptance criteria:
- Works in TTY environments with sensible fallback error when EDITOR missing.
- Preserves existing CLI args for non-interactive use.
- Template includes optional headings for Context/Scope/Acceptance Criteria.
close_reason: Implemented: --edit flag on create/update opens $EDITOR with template

title: trx-cli: Command-line interface
description: Implement the trx CLI with essential commands. Minimal but complete. Must support --json output for scripting and agent integration.

title: CLI: init command - create .trx directory
description: trx init [--prefix PREFIX]. Creates .trx/ directory with config.toml and empty issues.jsonl. Detect if already initialized.

title: CLI: create command - new issues
description: trx create TITLE [-t TYPE] [-p PRIORITY] [-d DESC] [--parent ID]. Create new issue, print ID. Support --json output.

title: CLI: list command - show issues
description: trx list [--status STATUS] [--type TYPE] [--all]. List issues in table format. Default shows open only. Support --json output.

title: CLI: show command - issue details
description: trx show ID. Display full issue details including description, dependencies, timestamps. Support --json output.

title: CLI: update command - modify issues
description: trx update ID [--status STATUS] [--priority P] [--title TITLE] [--desc DESC]. Update issue fields. Support --json output.

title: CLI: close command - close issues
description: trx close ID [-r REASON]. Close issue with optional reason. Support --json output.

title: CLI: ready command - show unblocked work
description: trx ready. Show open issues with no open blocking dependencies. Sorted by priority. Support --json output.

title: CLI: dep command - manage dependencies
description: trx dep add ID --blocks OTHER. trx dep rm ID --blocks OTHER. trx dep tree ID. Manage issue dependencies.

title: CLI: sync command - git add/commit .trx
description: trx sync [-m MESSAGE]. Git add and commit .trx/ directory. Simple wrapper, no complex merge logic - let git handle it.

title: Add list filters (status/type/priority/search)
description: Improve trx list ergonomics with flags like --status open,closed --type bug,task --priority 0..4 and --search text. Support combined filters.
close_reason: Implemented: trx list --priority P, --search TEXT, filters combinable

title: trx ready should show all unblocked issues, not just epics - leaf feature/task issues with no blockers should appear
close_reason: Fixed: ready now shows all unblocked open issues regardless of type; also shows blocked issues with their blockers

title: Custom ID prefix on create - 'trx create --id mmry-lrn' to control the generated issue ID prefix instead of random
close_reason: Custom --id prefix on create

title: Feature: trx create many (batch mode) from stdin/JSON for scripting agents
description:
Add batch issue creation mode for agent automation and migration tasks.

Request:
- trx create many --json-input issues.json
- trx create many --json-input -   # stdin

Input shape example:
[{
  "title": "...",
  "issue_type": "task",
  "priority": 2,
  "description": "...",
  "parent": "trx-abc",
  "blocks": ["trx-x","trx-y"]
}]

Acceptance criteria:
- Creates all issues transactionally per item with clear per-item status in output.
- Returns mapping from temp index/title to created IDs.
- Supports --dry-run preview and validation errors without partial chaos.
close_reason: Implemented: trx create-many --json-input with stdin support and --dry-run

title: Add structured verification evidence and configurable issue-closure gates
description:
## Problem

Issues can currently be closed even when explicit acceptance criteria have not been proven. Test commands, screenshots, browser traces, videos, CI runs, and deployment evidence are usually left as unstructured prose, making it difficult for humans or agents to determine whether a fix was verified against the current revision.

TRX should provide a generic, structured verification contract without becoming a test runner or binary artifact store.

## Desired outcome

Add first-class verification records that attach proof metadata and artifact references to issues, plus optional repository policy that prevents closure when required verification is missing, failing, or stale.

This must remain repository/tool agnostic. Oqto E2E runs are the first concrete consumer, but TRX must not depend on Oqto, Playwright, a particular CI provider, or a particular artifact store.

## Proposed domain model

An issue may have zero or more immutable verification-run records containing at least:

- stable run ID
- issue ID
- status: `passed`, `failed`, `error`, or `skipped`
- subject revision, normally a Git commit SHA
- timestamp
- environment/target, e.g. `local`, `ubuntu-worker`, `ci`
- scenario/check identifiers
- command or producer identity
- concise summary
- artifact references as URIs or paths
- optional structured checks with name/status/detail
- optional known gaps

Verification records should be append-only audit facts. A later run supersedes an earlier run for policy evaluation but does not erase history.

## Proposed CLI

Exact spelling may be refined for consistency with the existing CLI, but the intended workflow is:

```bash
trx verify add <issue-id> \
  --run-id e2e-20260715-abc123 \
  --status passed \
  --revision abc1234 \
  --environment ubuntu-worker \
  --scenario chat-reliability \
  --command "just e2e --target ubuntu-worker --scenario chat-reliability --video" \
  --artifact "file://artifacts/e2e-20260715-abc123"

trx verify list <issue-id>
trx verify show <issue-id> <run-id>
```

Support `--json` for add/list/show. Consider stdin/JSON input so CI and test runners can submit complete records without fragile shell argument construction.

## Configurable closure policy

Repositories should be able to opt into closure requirements, for example:

```toml
[verification]
require_for = ["bug", "feature"]
require_pass = true
require_current_revision = true
```

Policy may later support required scenario/check names, but v1 should stay small. When policy is enabled, `trx close <id>` must fail with actionable diagnostics if:

- no qualifying verification exists;
- the latest qualifying verification failed or errored;
- `require_current_revision` is enabled and the proof targets an older revision.

Provide an explicit auditable override, for example:

```bash
trx close <id> --verification-override "Accepted documentation-only follow-up by Tommy"
```

The override reason must be persisted in the event log and visible in issue history/show output.

## Boundaries / non-goals

TRX must not:

- execute tests or browser automation;
- capture screenshots or videos;
- upload/store binary artifacts inside `.trx`;
- validate that arbitrary remote artifact URLs remain available forever;
- depend on Oqto or any CI vendor;
- infer verification success from issue-description prose.

TRX stores compact structured metadata and references. The producing test system owns execution and artifacts.

## Compatibility and persistence

- Existing repositories and issues must continue to work unchanged when verification policy is absent.
- Existing `trx close` behavior remains unchanged by default.
- New event/data fields must deserialize safely in old repositories using defaults.
- JSONL/event-log semantics must remain append-only and merge-friendly.
- Duplicate submission of the same issue/run ID should be idempotent or fail clearly without creating ambiguous records.

## Positive proof / acceptance criteria

### Data model and serialization

- [ ] Verification records round-trip through the canonical store/event log.
- [ ] Existing repositories without verification records load unchanged.
- [ ] Duplicate run-ID behavior is deterministic and covered by tests.

### CLI

- [ ] `trx verify add/list/show` work in human-readable and JSON modes.
- [ ] CI can submit a complete verification record through structured JSON or stdin.
- [ ] Invalid issue IDs, statuses, revisions, and malformed artifact references produce actionable errors and no partial write.

### Closure gate

- [ ] With no verification config, existing close behavior is unchanged.
- [ ] A configured bug/feature cannot close without qualifying passing evidence.
- [ ] Failed/error evidence blocks closure.
- [ ] Stale-revision evidence blocks closure when `require_current_revision = true`.
- [ ] A passing run for the current revision permits closure.
- [ ] An explicit override permits closure and persists its reason as an auditable event.

### End-to-end proof

Add an integration test using a temporary Git/TRX repository that:

1. creates a bug;
2. enables verification policy;
3. proves close fails without evidence;
4. adds a failed run and proves close still fails;
5. adds a passing run for an older commit and proves stale evidence fails;
6. adds a passing run for current HEAD and proves close succeeds;
7. separately proves an override closes and the reason is visible in JSON/history output;
8. verifies referenced artifact files are not copied into `.trx`.

Run and record the repository's normal format, lint, unit, integration, and warning-free gates.

## Design questions to settle during implementation

- Store verification records as dedicated event variants, issue projections, or both.
- Define revision semantics in non-Git or dirty-worktree contexts.
- Decide whether scenario requirements belong in v1 or a later policy extension.
- Define URI/path normalization without turning TRX into an artifact validator.

title: trx-tui: Terminal UI viewer
description: Ratatui-based TUI that replaces beads-viewer. Interactive issue browser with graph visualization, PageRank scoring, and cross-repo workspace support.

title: TUI: Issue list view with filtering and sorting
description: Main list view showing issues. Filter by status/type/priority. Sort by priority, date, PageRank score. Vim-style navigation (j/k/g/G).

title: TUI: Issue detail panel
description: Side panel or modal showing full issue details. Title, description, dependencies, timestamps. Scrollable content.

title: TUI: Dependency graph visualization
description: ASCII/Unicode graph view showing dependency relationships. Highlight cycles, critical path, blocked chains.
close_reason: Deferred: TUI not primary interface, CLI dep tree covers the need

title: TUI: Multi-repo workspace support
description: Load workspace.yaml with multiple repos. Aggregate view across repos. Support beads-viewer workspace format for compatibility.
close_reason: Deferred: TUI not primary interface, multi-repo not prioritized

title: TUI: Robot mode JSON output for automation
description: trx tui --robot-triage, --robot-next, --robot-insights. JSON output for CI/automation. Compatible with beads-viewer robot mode.

title: Resolve store root at git boundary to prevent cross-repo writes
description:
Problem
- `Store::open()` currently searches upward from CWD until it finds a `.trx` directory.
- In nested/multi-repo workspaces, running `trx` in a repo without local `.trx` can silently write into a parent repo's tracker.
- This is surprising and can pollute the wrong issue store.

Reproduction
1. Have a parent directory with `.trx/`.
2. Create or enter a child git repo (or project dir) without `.trx/`.
3. Run `trx create "test"` in child repo.
4. Observe issue is created in parent `.trx`.

Expected
- By default, store discovery should not cross repository boundaries.
- If no `.trx` exists in the current git repo root (or below), command should fail with a clear "not initialized" error and guidance to run `trx init`.

Proposed behavior
Option A (recommended)
- Discover git repo root first.
- Search for `.trx` only between CWD and that repo root (inclusive).
- If none found: `NotInitialized`.

Option B
- Keep current behavior but add explicit opt-in for parent traversal outside git root (e.g. `--global-store` or env var).

UX additions
- Add `trx info` note when fallback/non-local resolution is used.
- Consider `trx --root <path>` / `TRX_ROOT` override for explicit targeting.

Acceptance criteria
- Running trx commands in a repo without `.trx` does not mutate parent repo store by default.
- Tests cover discovery behavior with nested dirs and git boundaries.
- Error/help text points to `trx init` and `trx info`.
close_reason: Implemented: Store::find_root now stops .trx discovery at git repo boundary; added boundary tests; updated CHANGELOG (Unreleased/Fixed). Verified with cargo test -p trx-core test_find_root_.

title: trx handover - generate compact one-line summary of all issues with dependency order for agent handoff
close_reason: Added trx handover command with compact one-line format

title: trx list --json should include resolved parent, blocked_by, blocks fields - currently these are null even when dependencies exist
close_reason: Implemented: trx list --json now includes resolved parent, blocked_by, blocks, children fields

title: Auto-resolve ISSUES.md merge conflicts
description: ISSUES.md is generated from individual .trx/issues/*.toml files, so merge conflicts in ISSUES.md should be auto-resolvable. Add a 'trx resolve' or 'trx fix' CLI command that regenerates ISSUES.md from the source issue files, resolving any git merge conflicts. This should also be hookable as a git merge driver so conflicts never appear in the first place.
close_reason: Implemented trx resolve, trx merge-driver install/uninstall/status

title: Support description input from file or stdin
description: For long issue text, allow trx create/update to read description from --description-file or --description - (stdin).
close_reason: Covered by v0.3.0: --edit flag opens $EDITOR, create-many reads from stdin

title: Add --assignee filter to list
description: Issues have an assignee field but list cannot filter by it. Add --assignee flag. Support --assignee me for current user (from git config or AGENT_HARNESS env).
close_reason: Added --assignee filter with 'me' resolution

title: Agent Ergonomics Pack: planning, dependency UX, and bulk authoring
description:
Meta epic to improve trx ergonomics for agent-driven planning and execution workflows.

Child candidates:
- trx-yy3z (dependency type integrity bug)
- trx-btfs (plan import scaffolding)
- trx-80by (directional dependency commands)
- trx-ne4f (external editor create/update)
- trx-pe3m (batch create from JSON/stdin)

Outcome target:
- Faster epic decomposition
- Lower command cognitive load
- More reliable dependency semantics
- Better long-form issue authoring ergonomics
close_reason: All 5 children implemented: yy3z (bug fix), 80by (dep block/unblock), pe3m (batch create), btfs (plan import), ne4f (editor workflow)

title: Standardize list/search JSON output for cross-tool integration
description: For unified search across hstry/mmry/trx, results need a common envelope format: { source: 'trx', source_repo, id, title, content (description), status, priority, labels, created_at, metadata }. Ensure trx list --json output matches this schema so agntz can merge results from all three tools.
close_reason: Search JSON output uses standardized envelope format

title: Bug: trx dep add mutates existing parent_child relationship type to blocks
description:
Observed when creating child issues with --parent and later adding dependencies via 'trx dep add'.

Expected:
- Existing parent_child relationship should be preserved as type=parent_child.
- Additional dependency edges should be added independently.

Actual:
- After dep add, existing parent linkage in output appears as type=blocks (semantic drift).

Why this matters:
- Parent/child vs blocker relationships have different semantics for hierarchy and readiness.
- Tooling and UI that rely on dependency type become inaccurate after edits.

Acceptance criteria:
- Repro test added for create --parent + dep add sequence.
- Parent edge type remains parent_child after any dep operations.
- Existing data migration/normalization for corrupted edge types if needed.
close_reason: Fixed: DependencyType Display now uses snake_case matching serde; add_dependency prevents duplicates to same target; CRDT store uses FromStr for dep type parsing
